page: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARMv8 architecture powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor. This configuration provides 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography but lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, or BMI2. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates a distinct performance profile characterized by strong multi-threaded capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test registers a strong score of 85,033.61, and software compilation via Clang also shows strong performance at 40,274.0. Conversely, single-core benchmarks are relatively weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,118.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1,822.78. Single-threaded data compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, also fall into the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth remains consistent, measuring 1436.97 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 139.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

With its 48 dedicated physical cores and 192.0 GB of RAM, the D48ps_v5 offers a cost-efficient resource density for multi-threaded workloads that do not rely on x86 vector instructions. It is suited for software compilation, static web serving (achieving a throughput of 26.42 GB/sec), and database operations, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 21,371.02 and Redis SET throughput of 30.96 million operations per second. The server can also handle moderate LLM inference tasks, processing 54.12 tokens per second for a 7B model prompt, though it is less efficient for larger 70B models.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, powered by a 48-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 85,033.61 and strong Clang software compilation results. However, single-core performance is weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,118.0. The architecture lacks AVX and AMX extensions but supports AES cryptography. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, offering a balanced resource density without the overhead of hyperthreading.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is a 48-core ARM-based server with 192 GB RAM. It features strong multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations, though single-core execution is weaker.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is a 48-core ARM-based virtual machine with 192 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based forty-eight core virtual machine delivering strong multi-threaded performance and balanced memory allocation for scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARMv8 Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores at 3.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Lacks local storage and integrated GPU accelerators
Weaker single-core and single-threaded compression performance
